summary: Independent read-only QA R5 accepts M3-POSTWAR-GOVERNANCE-001. The prior same-district different-victor stale-rights finding and later direct-control-first R4 blocker are closed. R5 uses a district-keyed condition-based defensive-garrison marker for direct-control arrangements; later same-district postwar commands reject before mutation, independent of payload.localPolityId.

decisions.decision: Accept M3 postwar governance after R5 direct-control marker fix.
decisions.rationale: Duplicate or replacement governance for the same district now rejects before mutation across same-victor, different-victor, localPolityId bypass, and direct-control-first paths; required deterministic tests and full package/root checks pass.

acceptance_checks: Three governance methods exist: direct-control, restore-vassal-ruler, tribute-only.
Immediate previews expose administrative burden, obligation shape, expected income/tribute, local acceptance/reliability, military readiness/risk, and reason codes.
24-month deterministic comparison shows materially different income, tribute, administrative load, reliability/risk, and military contribution outcomes.
State changes use explicit sim.apply-m3-postwar-governance GameCommand with sim.m3-postwar-governance-applied event; previews/comparisons use explicit query kinds.
Same-district repeated arrangements now reject across same-victor and different-victor cases, including payload.localPolityId bypass attempts.
Rejected commands preserve state hash, controller, administrative districts, obligations, enfeoffments, and suzerain records.
Restore-vassal-ruler still rejects missing ruler or policy.
Direct-control marker is condition-based with zero nominal/due/arrears accounting and does not create resources.

tests_run.result: status REVIEW, route_to systems_architect, risk R3.
33 changed/untracked paths all inside allowed_paths; none matched forbidden_paths.
No matches in touched implementation/test surfaces; expected no-match exit.
No matches in touched implementation/test surfaces; expected no-match exit.

tests_run.command: inline R5 postwar guard repro matrix
tests_run.result: Vassal-first different-victor/localPolityId bypass and direct-control-first duplicate/replacement cases rejected unchanged; marker condition accounting is zero.
architecture passed; root 24 files/157 tests; web 1 file/3 tests.

scope_assessment.scope_creep: No M4 campaign/marching/battle/siege/supply/reinforcement, full diplomacy/treaty/legitimacy/rebellion, client UI, content-source, save-format migration, historical content, dependency, or lockfile drift found.
scope_assessment.dependency_diff: Only packages/sim-core/package.json adds tests/sim-core-m3-postwar-governance.test.ts to the sim-core test script.

risks.issue: Direct-control postwar persistence uses an existing obligation marker rather than a first-class postwar arrangement table. Future replacement/retirement semantics should be designed separately before allowing replacement.
Projection coefficients remain M3 prototype validation constants, not final balance.

requested_action: Lead may proceed with acceptance/closure for M3-POSTWAR-GOVERNANCE-001.
